name: thurston_cusped_volume_as_dehn_filling_limit
value_exact: Vol(4_1) = 2*V_reg_tet = (3*sqrt3/2)*L(chi_{-3},2), sup limit of Vol(4_1(p,q)) as fillings lengthen (Thurston's hyperbolic Dehn surgery theorem)
banked_cell: knowledge/K004_figure_eight_dehn_filling_apolynomials.md:20-22 ('By Thurston's hyperbolic Dehn surgery theorem all but finitely many fillings stay hyperbolic, with volumes increasing to the cusped volume vol(4_1)=2.0299... from below'); computationally verified frontier/B96_geometry_invariants/FINDINGS.md:22-26 ('of 156 nontrivial fillings... 155 are strictly below vol_0, 0 exceed it - the Neumann-Zagier Hessian... negative definite'); exact L-value identity frontier/B680_arithmetic_meditation (docs/LAW_MAP.md row 'THE VOLUME IS THE BEING-CHARACTER L-VALUE': 'Vol(4_1)=(3sqrt3/2)L(chi_-3,2) exactly, verified 40 digits')
forced: YES - the complete hyperbolic structure is UNIQUE by Mostow-Prasad rigidity, and Thurston's theorem forces monotone convergence of Dehn-filled volumes to this specific number regardless of which slope sequence is sent to infinity; the limit value itself is choice-free (the CHOICE lives only in which finite (p,q) filling one inspects en route, not in the limit).
field: transcendental (Borel regulator / Bloch-Wigner dilogarithm value in the Bloch group of Q(sqrt-3); = (3sqrt3/2) L(chi_{-3},2))

name: neumann_zagier_quadratic_correction_constant
value_exact: pi/sqrt3 = 2*pi/|tau_cusp|, tau_cusp = 2*sqrt3*i; coefficient of the 1/n^2 term in ell_C(1,n) = 2*pi*i/n + (pi/sqrt3)/n^2 + O(1/n^3)
banked_cell: frontier/B290_core_scale_ladder/FINDINGS.md:9-18 ('The 1/n^2 correction coefficient is pi/sqrt3 = 2*pi/|tau_cusp|, with the cusp shape tau_cusp = 2*sqrt3*i... matches the measured Re(ell_C)*n^2 -> 1.81379 to 5 digits'); the 1/n^3 imaginary term pi/6 also recorded there
forced: PARTIALLY - the coefficient pi/sqrt3 is a fixed invariant of the (rigid, choice-free) complete cusp shape, extracted as the n->infinity asymptotic Taylor coefficient of the core-geodesic length; but B290's own caveat (line 20-24) notes the tidy REAL form is figure-eight-specific (because tau_cusp happens to be purely imaginary) and the general formula is ell_C = 2*pi*i/n + (2*pi*i/tau)/n^2 + ...; also the (1,n) meridian-filling family is a specific choice of surgery curve (canonical for a knot's meridian, but the family index n itself is CHOICE-PARAMETERIZED, see note below). The coefficient is forced; the surgery family exhibiting it is a natural but not unique diagnostic.
field: transcendental (pi-type), coefficient built from tau_cusp in Q(sqrt-3)

name: systole_complex_length
value_exact: lambda = 1.08707... - 1.72277...i (shortest geodesic of the complete hyperbolic structure), trace x = 2*cosh(lambda/2) = 2-omega = (3-i*sqrt3)/2
banked_cell: frontier/B559_blackhole_probes/FINDINGS.md:52-62 (table: 'Re lambda=1.08707, Im lambda=-1.72277, trace x=2-omega=(3-i*sqrt3)/2, Re(S)=Re(lambda)/2=0.5435 (systole)'); cross-referenced as the modular-surface analogue in frontier/B96_geometry_invariants/FINDINGS.md:18-20 ('m=1 is the systole... two independent simplicity measures agree') and frontier/B470_reflection_campaign/FINDINGS.md:104 ('systole stays bounded (1.087, 0.633, 0.697, 0.666)')
forced: YES - the systole (shortest element of the discrete length spectrum) of the UNIQUE complete hyperbolic structure (Mostow-Prasad rigidity) is a rigid geometric invariant; it is the literal minimum over an intrinsically-ordered, choice-free set (the whole length spectrum), so extracting 'the shortest one' involves no selection.
field: trace x=(3-i*sqrt3)/2 is algebraic in Q(sqrt-3) (an Eisenstein integer, 2-omega); the length lambda=2*arccosh(x/2) itself is transcendental-log-type over Q(sqrt-3)
